DECLARATION OF GUY W. STILSON IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT ANNA GATTIS MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 63134818) FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOW, BALL & LYNCH, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF JUDGMENT, IF ENTERED CCP 473.5, CCP 473(D) AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION OF ANNA GATTI (TRANSACTION ID # 100068603) (TRANSACTION ID # 100068603) FILED BY DEFENDANT GATTI, ANNA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GATTI'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON APRIL 17, 2018 AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED ON SEPTEMBER 27, 2018 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EFFECTUATE PROPER SERVICE AND THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE VOID PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(D). (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS) PLAINTIFF PROPERLY NOTES THAT THE SERVICE PROVISIONS ARE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED WHERE ACTUAL NOTICE HAS OCCURRED. (SEE DILL V. BERQUIST CONSTRUCTION CO. (1994) 24 CAL.APP.4TH 1426, 1436.) IN CONTRAST, "'[I]N ORDER TO OBTAIN IN PERSONAM JURISDICTION THROUGH ANY FORM OF CONSTRUCTIVE SERVICE THERE MUST BE STRICT COMPLIANCE WITH THE REQUISITE STATUTORY PROCEDURES.'" (ZIRBES V. STRATTON (1986) 186 CAL.APP.3D 1407, 1416.) IN THIS CASE, MS. GATTI DID NOT RECEIVE ACTUAL NOTICE OF THIS ACTION IN TIME TO DEFEND. (GATTI DECL. 19-22.) THE COURT THEREFORE GRANTS THE MOTION TO VACATE AND QUASHES SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Motion Of Prove Up Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing and Entry of Default Judgment as to defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Lisa Schafer, CSR #12723. Telephone: 530-867-4259. Email: lschafer@sftc.org.

Lorna Walker, Esq. of Law Offices of Sweet & Walker, PC appeared for plaintiff Low, Ball & Lynch, A Professional Law Corporation.
Sonja Blomquist, Dissolution Manager for plaintiff, Low, Ball & Lynch, A Professional Law Corporation, also present, was placed under oath and testified.
Mses Walker and Blomquist both appeared via ZOOM Video Conference for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Account Stated and Quantum Merit was filed on September 7, 2017.
Substitute Service on Summons and Complaint on defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC on September 15, 2017 and was effectuated on March 16, 2018.
Default as to both defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC was entered on April 17, 2018.
Order granting defendant Anna Gatti's Motion to Set-Aside the Default was filed on April 18, 2019.
Plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and Accompanying Documents were personally served on defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC on January 28, 2020.
Default as to both defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC was entered on July 17, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 12, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Low, Ball & Lynch, A Professional Law Corporation, and against defendants Anna Gatti and IQSystem, LLC, as follows:

Principal/Damages - $380,252.36. (Amount pled in complaint).
Prejudgment Interest - $124,701.94.
Costs - $361.85.

Total - $505,316.15.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Motion Of Prove Up Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Dec-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgement And Recall And Quash Writ Of Judgment, If Entered And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James J. Patrick, Esq., Low Ball & Lynch, 415-981-6630, appearing for Plaintiff;
Anna Gatti, 415-697-9026, appearing on her own behalf as Pro Per Defendant; (CourtCall)

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Gatti's motion to vacate the default entered on April 17, 2018 and default judgment entered on September 27, 2018 is granted. Plaintiff failed to effectuate proper service and the default and default judgment are void pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d).

Plaintiff did not properly substitute serve Ms. Gatti at the 690 Market St., #2401 address. Even if she or a related trust owned that property, the undisputed evidence shows that the premises was never her "dwelling house, usual place of abode, usual place of business, or usual mailing address." (Code Civ. Proc. 415.20(b); Gatti Decl., 7-8, 9, 15.) Plaintiff was on notice that she had moved to Italy in June 2017, and did not request her new mailing address. "[A] default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void. Whether the lack of jurisdiction appears on the face of the judgment roll, or is shown by extrinsic evidence for a judgment that appears valid on its face, in either case the judgment is for all purposes a nullity-past, present and future." (OC Interior Services, LLC v. Nationstar Mortgage, LLC (2017) 7 Cal.App.5th 1318, 1331 (citations and internal quotations omitted).)

Plaintiff properly notes that the service provisions are liberally construed where actual notice has occurred. (See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1436.) In contrast, "'[i]n order to obtain in personam jurisdiction through any form of constructive service there must be strict compliance with the requisite statutory procedures.'" (Zirbes v. Stratton (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 1407, 1416.) In this case, Ms. Gatti did not receive actual notice of this action in time to defend. (Gatti Decl. 19-22.)

The Court therefore grants the motion to vacate and quashes service of the summons and complaint.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
